findings.summary: Deferred obligation: wire the committed stub workflow under spikes/dafny-compat/ci/ as a permanent CI job when the CI feature lands (spike suite becomes CI-enforced). The wired job MUST run FROM-CLEAN (rm -rf out / fresh checkout, no cached out/runid-* run roots — a warm out/ masks the AP-021 circular-gate class; this net's absence let both PMB-001 and PMB-002 escape). The stub encodes this (QA-023 note 5), test-asserted. Dischargeable only by the CI feature's spec.
findings.resolution: Realized as a LIVE GitHub Actions job at .github/workflows/dafny-compat-spike.yml (checkout fetch-depth:0 per QA-023 note 1; pinned SDK 10.0.302 installed to $HOME/.dotnet so the documented bare root command resolves it under env -i; `rm -rf spikes/dafny-compat/out` FROM-CLEAN; controller exit is the gate). The former stub spikes/dafny-compat/ci/spike-ci.yml is retained as the co-located requirements charter (test: Inv014OperatorSurfaceTests.CiWorkflow_Realized_LiveJobAndCharter_EncodeFromCleanGate). PENDING: the committed 1800s wall-clock bound must be confirmed sufficient on the ubuntu-latest runner class by the first live run — any bound change goes through DD-006, never a silent edit. Caching (out/cache only) deliberately omitted for a strictly from-clean first wiring.

findings.summary: Deferred obligation: promote ADR-0001 from provisional to accepted (or supersede with counter-evidence) — inherited by the final Phase 0.0 feature's spec.

findings.title: Uncovered exit/report-matrix cell: child exit 20 + all-pass report maps to COMPATIBLE/exit-0
findings.detail: MapExit default branch assumes a typed cause without checking the report; Aggregate discards the outcome and computes the verdict from the report alone. No current code path can produce the contradiction (child exit derives from the same Results list its report is emitted from); controller exit remains fail-closed. Fix: one MapExit branch + one additive matrix row + one induced test.
findings.disposition: user-deferred at QA round 3 (final) disposition gate, 2026-07-22

findings.title: The MA-UC-4 fix bound the project-enforcement perimeter across 3 copies (sln <-> SpikePath
findings.detail: The MA-UC-4 fix bound the project-enforcement perimeter across 3 copies (sln <-> SpikePaths.Projects <-> run-spike.sh lock-receipt list) but left a 4th unbound: the aggregator's SpikeProjects() (Program.cs), which drives the per-project lockDigests in run-report binding. Consistent today, but on a project addition the sln<->SpikePaths test goes red while SpikeProjects() silently omits the new project's lock digest from evidence - a fail-open narrowing of INV-001 lock-pin binding no test catches.  FIX: Extend the Uc4 test to assert the aggregator's SpikeProjects() name+path set == SpikePaths.Projects. The 'one authoritative source with equality tests against each copy' rule must enumerate ALL copies incl. aggregator-side.
findings.disposition: deferred (round-2 mini-audit LOW; operator-hardening/honesty/latent — no verdict-integrity impact)

findings.title: env-profiles.json still declares SSL_CERT_FILE/SSL_CERT_DIR as <passthrough> for controlle
findings.detail: env-profiles.json still declares SSL_CERT_FILE/SSL_CERT_DIR as <passthrough> for controller/provision/fetch/restore/build/test, but MA-HI-1's env -i strips SSL_CERT_* before the inner controller runs, so build_profile_env's passthrough branch always resolves empty. The launches never receive SSL_CERT_*. Not exploitable/fail-closed (fetches are SHA-256-pinned) but the profile advertises a passthrough the MA-HI-1 contract guarantees can never fire, plus a latent portability failure on minimal hosts.  FIX: Drop the two dead <passthrough> keys from the affected classes, OR add SSL_CERT_FILE/DIR to the MA-HI-1 re-exec allowlist if passthrough is wanted. An env-i allowlist and the profile passthrough set must be reconciled by a static check.
findings.disposition: deferred (round-2 mini-audit LOW; operator-hardening/honesty/latent — no verdict-integrity impact)

findings.title: The suite receipt carries source_manifest_sha256 (emitted by run-spike.sh, in the Contract
findings.detail: The suite receipt carries source_manifest_sha256 (emitted by run-spike.sh, in the ContractTypes contract) but the aggregator reads only run_id/nonce/suite_exit - the field is never validated. Not a hole (run_id+nonce already bind the receipt to this run) but it READS like a source-identity binding while providing none (MA-VI-6's 'source-manifest digest binding' is carried but not enforced).  FIX: Cross-check source_manifest_sha256 against run-context.json's source_set_sha256 so the receipt genuinely attests the source the suite validated, OR drop the field. A receipt field emitted but never consumed by its validator should be enforced or removed.
findings.disposition: deferred (round-2 mini-audit LOW; operator-hardening/honesty/latent — no verdict-integrity impact)

findings.title: The MA-HI-3 (json_escape control chars) fix is locked only by a source-scan asserting the
findings.detail: The MA-HI-3 (json_escape control chars) fix is locked only by a source-scan asserting the substitution strings \n/\t/\r appear in the function body. A semantic escaping bug that keeps those strings but mis-orders substitutions (a later backslash-escape double-escaping) would emit invalid JSON and stay green - a hostile-input finding where a cheap behavioral test was feasible.  FIX: Feed a --run-root path (or report value) containing a raw newline through the real write_failed_report path and JsonDocument.Parse the emitted report. Hostile-input JSON-emission fixes get a round-trip (emit->parse) test, never a scan of the escaper body alone.
findings.disposition: deferred (round-2 mini-audit LOW; operator-hardening/honesty/latent — no verdict-integrity impact)

findings.title: The aggregator's --nonce is optional (only --run-id required); every receipt check is 'rec
findings.detail: The aggregator's --nonce is optional (only --run-id required); every receipt check is 'receiptRunId != runId || (nonce.Length>0 && receiptNonce != nonce)', so omitting --nonce degrades all receipt binding (suite/restore/build) to run_id-only, and run_id is not secret (it appears in every on-disk report/receipt). Does not breach MA-VI-6's threat (presence of a suite receipt with suite_exit==0 is the real gate; forging needs run-root write access) but the class-fix intent 'receipt-bound to the run mint, never a bare argv claim' is only as strong as run_id unless --nonce is mandatory.  FIX: Require a non-empty --nonce in the aggregator so binding degrades closed rather than silently to run_id.
findings.disposition: deferred (round-2 mini-audit LOW; operator-hardening/honesty/latent — no verdict-integrity impact)

findings.title: The aggregator terminal summary (advertised in README as 'verdict, failed probes with reas
findings.detail: The aggregator terminal summary (advertised in README as 'verdict, failed probes with reasons, report path - no JSON parsing needed') prints 'route A verdict: INCOMPLETE; failed probes: -' whenever all probes pass but final_suite_status != success (every variance run + the MA-VI-6 canonical downgrade) - StateText renders bare INCOMPLETE with an empty failed-probes list. A summary-only reader sees an unexplained INCOMPLETE with no failed probes; the reason is only in the JSON verdict_reason. MA-VI-6's downgrade adds a canonical path to reach it.  FIX: Append the verdict reason to the summary line for non-probe-failure states, e.g. 'INCOMPLETE (suite-failure: final_suite_status=unknown)'. Any 'no JSON parsing needed' summary renders the reason for every non-COMPATIBLE state.
findings.disposition: deferred (round-2 mini-audit LOW; operator-hardening/honesty/latent — no verdict-integrity impact)

findings.title: run-spike.sh/regen-sample.sh refuse an unhardened (no -p) invocation with exit 30; clean-r
findings.detail: run-spike.sh/regen-sample.sh refuse an unhardened (no -p) invocation with exit 30; clean-runs.sh refuses the same class with exit 20; provision-z3.sh has NO -p self-check at all. Docs are consistent (no doc-vs-code contradiction) but a CI wrapper keying on '30=refused-unhardened vs 20=usage' misclassifies clean-runs' hardening refusal, and standalone provision-z3.sh runs unhardened silently.  FIX: Make clean-runs use exit 30 for the unhardened refusal, add the same -p check + exit 30 to provision-z3.sh. One shared 'refuse unhardened -> exit 30' idiom across all four scripts, asserted by a static test.
findings.disposition: deferred (round-2 mini-audit LOW; operator-hardening/honesty/latent — no verdict-integrity impact)

findings.title: The MA-ED-3 fix_note states the Ed3 test asserts 'no rooted host path (/home,/Users,/tmp,/
findings.detail: The MA-ED-3 fix_note states the Ed3 test asserts 'no rooted host path (/home,/Users,/tmp,/opt,/var)', but the implemented assertion only checks four concrete strings (runId, 'run_directory', runRoot, SpikeRoot). A stable system-rooted path (/opt/dotnet, /usr/share/dotnet) leaking into a class-2 value would not be caught. Determinism impact nil (such paths are run-stable; class-2 path-bearing fields are canonicalized + framework/system assemblies excluded). The code comment is accurate; only the audit-artifact fix_note overstates.  FIX: Align the MA-ED-3 fix_note wording to what Ed3 checks, OR add a generic rooted-path regex (^/(opt|var|usr|home|Users|tmp)/ or drive-letter) over the raw class-2 subtree. A 'no rooted path in class-2' assertion should test generically, not by enumerating known roots.
findings.disposition: deferred (round-2 mini-audit LOW; operator-hardening/honesty/latent — no verdict-integrity impact)

findings.title: list_descendant_sessions lacks the sess==candidate leader-guard its sibling find_session_l
findings.detail: list_descendant_sessions lacks the sess==candidate leader-guard its sibling find_session_leader has: for candidate==ancestor it emits sid[candidate] unconditionally. Harmless today because setsid -w (no -f, job control off) doesn't fork so WRAPPER_PID is the inner session leader. Latent: if the setsid call ever forks (adding -f, set -m, or setsid already a group leader), sid[WRAPPER_PID] becomes the OUTER session, and on operator cancel the trap kills -TERM/-KILL -<parent-session> - signaling the outer watchdog's own group, potentially killing the run before the OperatorCancelled synthetic is written. UNCERTAIN (not triggerable in the current invocation contract).  FIX: In list_descendant_sessions, continue when candidate==ancestor, or emit only when sid[candidate]==candidate (mirror find_session_leader). Session-sweep enumerators share one leader-identification helper.
findings.disposition: deferred (round-2 mini-audit LOW; operator-hardening/honesty/latent — no verdict-integrity impact)
